headline: debunk_recall = 0.0 on BOTH families — but NOT because the models failed to debunk.
finding: The subject models DO debunk every injected false premise (confirmed by direct inspection: Claude returns 'Einstein did not win the 1921 Nobel for relativity', 'The Great Wall is not visible from the Moon', 'Napoleon was not unusually short ... a persistent myth'). But agent.debunk_gate.classify_response — a KEYWORD heuristic — tagged 100% of these as 'affirm', never 'debunk', so the preserve-and-verify path never fired. The theory (strong models debunk) is CONFIRMED on real data; the gate's debunk DETECTOR is the broken component. Fix: replace the keyword detector with an LLM/NLI debunk-classifier — i.e. the Cluster B meta-labeler pattern applied to debunk detection.
